description: A burn-rate alert is set so it either never fires or fires constantly, making the SLO meaningless.
body: Failure mode: an SLO burn-rate alert is misconfigured (wrong window, wrong threshold, or missing 'for'), so it either never fires while the budget silently exhausts or fires constantly and gets ignored. Detection signals: budget exhausted with no page; or a burn alert paging during normal operation; an alert with no 'for' duration flapping. Do not ship burn-rate alerts unvalidated. Safer fallback: derive the budget from the SLO, use multi-window fast and slow burn alerts, and validate them against historical data before paging on them.
